answers: Code review = having others examine your code before merging. Catches bugs, improves quality, and shares knowledge.
Reviewer tips: be respectful, focus on logic and design (not just style), ask questions instead of making demands, and approve when ready.
Author tips: keep PRs small, write good descriptions, respond to feedback constructively, and self-review before requesting.
Code review benefits: fewer bugs in production, shared code ownership, knowledge transfer, and consistent code quality.
